comment.body:
☝️🟢It is mean reversion that happened today. I expected it for yesterday i dropped it on the chat, but it is only a mean reversion. We went up all week, catched intraday bottoms. 
Vol remains crushed into next week, long bias remains as long as we hold the mean that you can read from the indicators on daily. 
This is why I provide lvls and charts. Below the zero vanna and elevated vol you should have expected this and close the trade. 

Only bcs you potentially lost today, doesn't mean you are shit or this is shit. As wrote you at the beginning loss is the part of the game. 
Read my posts to learn about things, Take the time to properly digest them and discuss. It will be better. Self control and just go forward. 

Risk management is the part of the trading. Also dropped  post about that.

comment.body: Hi everybody! I hope everyone had a good week, and glad to see you are having fun, Alma. Has anyone else noticed that this decline seems to be coinciding w/ Cem Karsan's outlook about a stair step decline into March OpEx? Looking at the daily chart for $SPX, it looks like that is exactly what is happening- stair steps. We seem to be dropping every Friday on Trump bombs, and then climbing back up during the week, but, yes. Still lower highs as someone mentioned earlier. Just wondering if anyone else was noticing the same.

comment.body:
He had said back in January after the election gap fill on the 13th that if the market were to break new highs in the final weeks of January from that gap fill, then we’re likely to trend higher into March OPEX. 6300 is my guess 

Then if I remember correctly, if we couldn’t hold ~ 6100 into March OPEX or right after OPEX into the end of March, then we’d get a steeper, more rapid decline into 5200-5500. 

I believe this also aligns for the most part with what Alma has been saying/seeing. I think today was just noise. Everyone already knows about the tariffs. It wasn’t really “news.”

Russel 2000 broke last weeks high on Wednesday while S&P stayed in range. Russel while down, outpaced tech today. Seems risk on still, but undecided. Usually when the Russell is showing strength and holds overall trend after a flush like Monday, there’s not much downside for SPX. 

Would not shock me if we gap up Monday. Since we’ve had 2 straight weeks of tariffs on Friday, gap down Monday. Seems too easy. If we don’t gap up, maybe one last small flush on CPI just to make new highs into 2/21 like Alma has said. That’s my opinion
